recap:
- An embedding is a numerical representation of unstructured content, transforming high-dimensional data into lower-dimensional vectors that capture semantic meaning.
- Embeddings map similar concepts close together in vector space, allowing for the identification of conceptual relationships beyond exact keyword matches.
- While the focus is on unstructured text, embeddings can also represent images, audio, and other modalities, enabling cross-modal retrieval.
- Embedding models are specialized deep learning models that convert various data types into machine-readable numerical vectors.
- The choice of embedding model is crucial, as it significantly affects the quality of information retrieval and the alignment between query and document spaces.
